solution:
By observing the data given, by doubling the pressure, half-life is reduced to half. This result is following second order kinetics. Mathematically it can be proved as follows,

The half-life and initial pressure of nth order reaction can be related as follows,

t12∝Po1-n

t121t122=P011-nP021-n

⇒42=501001-n

⇒2=121-n

⇒2=2n-1

⇒n-1=1

⇒n=2

Hence, the order of the reaction is second order.

question: Which of the following cannot be explained by crystal field theory?
options: Magnetic properties of transition metal complexes
solution: The crystal field model is successful in explaining the formation, structures, colour and magnetic properties of coordination compounds to a large extent. However, from the assumptions that the ligands are point charges, it follows that anionic ligands should exert the greatest splitting effect. The anionic ligands actually are found at the low end of the spectrochemical series. So, it couldn't explain successfully the order of spectrochemical series.

question: The hybridization and magnetic behaviour of cobalt ion in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>[</mo><mi>Co</mi><mo>(</mo><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msub><mo>)</mo><mn>6</mn></msub><msup><mo>]</mo><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> complex, respectively is
solution:
In [Co(NH3)6]3+, Co is in +3 oxidation state with the configuration 3d6. In the presence of NH3 a strong field ligand, the 3d electrons undergo pairing and  leaving two d-orbitals empty. Hence, the hybridisation is d2sp3 which forms an inner orbital octahedral complex. The complex is diamagnetic as it has no unpaired electrons.

So, the correct option is C

question: Choose the correct representation of conductometric titration of benzoic acid vs sodium hydroxide.
solution:
C6H5CWAOOH+NaOSBH→C6H5CSaltOONa+H2O

 Initially the conductance is low due to the feeble ionisation of acetic acid. On the addition of base, there is decrease in conductance(A→B) not only due to the replacement of H+ by Na+ but also suppresses the dissociation of benzoic acid due to common ion benzoate. But very soon, the conductance increases(B→C) on adding NaOH as NaOH neutralises the un-dissociated C6H5COOH to C6H5COONa which is the strong electrolyte. This increase in conductance continues raise up to the equivalence point. The graph near the equivalence point is curved due the hydrolysis of salt C6H5COONa. Beyond the equivalence point, conductance increases(C→D) more rapidly with the addition of NaOH due to the highly conducting OH− ions

question:
Identify the correct statements about alkali metals.

A. The order of standard reduction potential (M+ | M) for alkali metal ions is Na > Rb > Li.

B. CsI is highly soluble in water.

C. Lithium carbonate is highly stable to heat.

D. Potassium dissolved in concentrated liquid ammonia is blue and paramagnetic.

E. All alkali metal hydrides are ionic solids.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution:
A. Electrode potential of given elements are:

 Li = -3.04  Na = -2.714  Rb = -2.98

The standard reduction potentials (E) of the alkali metals do not follow the trend based on ionisation energies. Unexpectedly, lithium is the strongest reductant, and sodium is the weakest. Therefore, reducing character sequences is Na <Rb < Li.

B. The low solubility of CsI is due to smaller hydration enthalpy of its two ions.

C. Li2CO3 decomposes readily to lithium monoxide and carbon dioxide.

D. Alkali metals are sodium, potassium which with liquid ammonia produce blue coloured solutions. Alkali metals dissolve in liquid ammonia to produce ammoniated electrons. It is paramagnetic due to the presence of unpaired electrons.

E.All the alkali metal hydrides are ionic solids with high melting points.

Hence, the correct option is A and E

q:
The number of statement's, which are correct with respect to the compression of carbon dioxide from point (a) in the Andrews isotherm from the following is _________.

A. Carbon dioxide remains as a gas upto point (b)
B. Liquid carbon dioxide appears at point (c)
C. Liquid and gaseous carbon dioxide coexist between points (b) and (c)
D. As the volume decreases from (b) to (c), the amount of liquid decreases
question:
The number of statement's, which are correct with respect to the compression of carbon dioxide from point (a) in the Andrews isotherm from the following is _________.

A. Carbon dioxide remains as a gas upto point (b)
B. Liquid carbon dioxide appears at point (c)
C. Liquid and gaseous carbon dioxide coexist between points (b) and (c)
D. As the volume decreases from (b) to (c), the amount of liquid decreases
solution:
Andrews noticed that at high temperatures isotherms look like that of an ideal gas and the gas cannot be liquefied even at very high pressure. As the temperature is lowered, shape of the curve changes and data shows considerable deviation from ideal behaviour.

Carbon dioxide remains as a gas only up to point b. 

At point b, liquid of a particular volume appears. Further compression does not change the pressure. Liquid and gaseous carbon dioxide coexist and further application of pressure results in the condensation of more gas until the point c is reached. At point c, all the gas has been condensed and further application of pressure merely compresses the liquid as shown by steep line. A point like d represents liquid state

solution:
Physisorption is reversible in nature. It has low adsorption enthalpy nearly 20 to 40 kJ/mol. It results in a multimolecular layer. It decreases with increase in temperature. Activation energy is less or zero in physisorption.

q: If the system of equations x+2y+3z=3, 4x+3y-4z=4 and 8x+4y-λz=9+μ has infinitely many solutions, then the ordered pair λ,μ is equal to
question: If the system of equations x+2y+3z=3, 4x+3y-4z=4 and 8x+4y-λz=9+μ has infinitely many solutions, then the ordered pair λ,μ is equal to
solution:
Given,

The system of equations 

x+2y+3z=3 ......1

4x+3y-4z=4 .........2

8x+4y-λz=9+μ .....3

Now using the operation, equation 1×4-2 we get,

5y+16z=8 ........4

Now using the operation, equation 2×2-3 we get,

2y+λ-8z=-1-μ ........5

Now using the operation, equation 4×2-3×5 we get,

72-5λz=21+5μ

Now given system of equation has infinitely many solutions, so making coefficient of z to be zero and equating 21+5μ with zero we get,

72-5λ=0⇒λ=725 and 21+5μ=0⇒μ=-215

Hence, the ordered pair λ,μ is equal to 725,-215.

question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>)</mo></math> be the solution of the differential equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>–</mo><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>)</mo><mi>dx</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>xy</mi><mo> </mo><mi>dy</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given equation is:

(x2– 3y2)dx+3xydy=0

We can re-write equation as

dydx=-x2-3y23xy

⇒dydx=yx-13xy   ....(1)

Put y=vx

dydx=v+xdvdx

Equation (1) can be written as

v+xdvdx=v-131v⇒vdv=-13x

Integrating both sides, we get

v22=-13lnx+c

⇒y22x2=-13lnx+c        .....(2)

∵ y1=1     (given)

∴ c=12       (from equation 2)

Equation (2) can be written as

y22x2=-13lnx+12

⇒y2=-23x2lnx+x2

Now y2(e)=-23e2lne+e2=-23e2+e2=e23

⇒6y2(e)=2e2

solution:

fx=22x22x+2,

so f1-x=22-2x22-2x+2=2222+22x+1=22+22x

Now fx+f1-x=22x22x+2+22+22x=1

f12023+f22023+f32023.........f20222023

question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> be a function such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>·</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>y</mi></mfenced></math> for all <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math>, If  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>1</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mi>n</mi></munderover><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>k</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>3279</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is
solution:
Given,

fx+y=fx·fy and f1=3 

Now taking x=1 & y=1 we get,

f1+1=f1·f1⇒f2=32

Similarly f2+1=f2·f1⇒f3=32×3=33

And so on fn=3n

So, ∑r=1nfr=3279

⇒f1+f2+f3.......fn=3279

⇒3+32+33.......+3n=3279

⇒3×3n-13-1=3279

⇒3n-12=1093

⇒3n-1=2186

⇒3n=2187

⇒3n=37

⇒n=7

solution:
Let

fx=x3-ax2+bx-c

f'x=3x2-2ax+b

f"x=6x-2a

f'''x=6

Also,

f'1=a

⇒3-2a+b=a

⇒3a=b+3

Also,

f"2=12-2a=b

⇒12-2a=3a-3

⇒5a=15⇒a=3

⇒b=6

And,

f'''3=c⇒c=6

Hence,

fx=x3-3x2+6x-6

Now,

f3+2f0=27-27+18-6-12=0

solution:
We have,

limx→ax-5-2x+2=0

⇒limx→ax-5-2x-2=0

⇒limx→ax-2x-7=0

⇒limx→ax-2x=7

Now when a∈I (integer) then a-2a=7⇒a=-7

Now when a∈I+f where f is fraction,

So, I+f-2I+2f=7

⇒I+f-2I-2f=7

⇒I+0-2I-2f=7

⇒-I-2f=7

Now taking case 1 when f∈0,12

Then ⇒-I-0=7⇒I=-7⇒a∈-7,-6.5

Now taking case 2 when f∈12,1

So, -I-1=7⇒I=-8⇒a∈-7.5,7

Hence, from both the cases we can say that a∈-7.5,-6.5.

q: The number of square matrices of order <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> with entries from the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>{</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>}</mo></math>, such that the sum of all the elements in each row is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> and the sum of all the elements in each column is also <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math>, is
question: The number of square matrices of order <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> with entries from the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>{</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>}</mo></math>, such that the sum of all the elements in each row is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> and the sum of all the elements in each column is also <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math>, is
solution:
Given,

A 5×5 matrix whose each entry is either 0 or 1, is such that sum of entries of each column as well as each row is 1,

Taking a possible case we get,

1000001000001000001000001

Now arranging the above case we get,

1000001000001000001000001→5 ways→4 ways→3 ways→2 ways→1 way because if we fix 1 at first row and first column then in second row first column 1 cannot come and vice-versa.

So total number of such matrices will be 5×4×3×2×1=120

Its vertex <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is on the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo></math>axis and its orthocentre is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. The length of the tangent from the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> to the part of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi></math> in the first quadrant is
question: The equations of sides <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>C</mi></math> of a triangle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mi>C</mi></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>λ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>λ</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>λ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>λ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> respectively. Its vertex <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is on the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo></math>axis and its orthocentre is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. The length of the tangent from the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> to the part of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi></math> in the first quadrant is
solution:
Given,

The equations of sides AB and AC of a triangle ABC and have orthocentre at 1,2

So, AB≡λ+1x+λy=4 ........1

And AC≡λx+1-λy+λ=0 ......2 

Also given vertex A is on the y-axis

So, putting the value x=0 in equation 1 & 2 we get,

y=4λ & y=λλ-1

⇒4λ=λλ-1

⇒λ=2

Now putting the value of λ in both equation we get,

AB≡3x+2y=4 ........3

AC≡2x-y+2=0 ......4

Now solving above equation we get, A0,2

Now let Cα,2α+2 {using equation 4}

Now we know that slopes of perpendicular lines is -1,

So, from diagram slope of altitude through C×slope of AB we get,

2αα-1×-32=-1

⇒α=-12

Hence, point C will be C-12,1

Now let the equation of tangent be y=mx+32m which passes through point C-12,1

So, 1=m-12+32m

⇒m2+2m-3=0

⇒m=1, -3

Also we know that point of contact of tangent to parabola is given by am2,2am≡32,3

Hence, CT=32+122+3-12=8=22

solution:
Case 1 

forming 4 digit number,

When 7 is at 1000's place

7 _ _ _,

So total number of ways will be 4×3×2=24 ways,

Case 2 

forming 4 digit number when 8 is at 1000's place 

8 _ _ _,

Case 3 

forming 5 digit number,

solution:
Given equation is:

3x2+1x2-2x+1x+5=0

We know,

x+1x2=x2+ 2+1x2   (using algebraic identities)

⇒x2+1x2=x+1x2-2

So, we can write the given equation as

3x+1x2-2-2x+1x+5=0

Let x+1x=t

⇒3t2-2-2t+5=0⇒3t2-6-2t+5=0

⇒3t2-2t-1=0⇒3t2-3t+t-1=0

⇒3tt-1+(t-1)=0⇒3t+1t-1=0

Therefore, t=-13 or t=1

Case I                                                     

x+1x=1                                                

⇒x2-x+1=0                                    

Here, D=-3 i.e. no real solution        

Case II x+1x=-13

⇒3x2+x+3=0

Here, D=-35 i.e. no real solution

Hence, we don't have real solutions.

solution:

R=a,b,b,c,b,d

For reflexivity, we must add a,a, b,b, c,c, d,d

solution:
We know that,

Direction ratio of line parallel the planes x+2y+z=0 and 3y-z=3 is given by,

D.R=i^j^k^12103-1=-5i^+j^+3k^

Now given line is passing through 3,2,1 and having direction ratio -5,1,3,

So, equation of line will be x-3-5=y-21=z-13=t

Now let any point line is given by Q≡-5t+3, t+2, 3t+1

Now we need to find foot of perpendicular from point P1,9,7,

So direction ratio of line QP will be -5t+3-1, t+2-9, 3t+1-7≡-5t+2, t-7, 3t-6

Now we know that QP will be perpendicular to the given line, so by condition of perpendicular lines we get,

-5t+2i^ +t-7j^ +3t-6k^·-5i^+j^+3k^=0

⇒25t-10+t-7+9t-18=0

⇒t=1

So, the point Q=-2,3,4, now comparing with  α,β,γ, we get α+β+γ=-2+3+4=5

solution:
Given equations are

x-λ3=y-264=z+265 is passing through a point λ,26,36 and it's direction ratios are 3,4,5.

So, 

a→1=λi^+26j^-26k^

And,

x+62=y-63=z-64 is passing through a point -6,6,6 and it's direction ratios are 1,2,3.

a→2=-6i^+6j^+6k^

Now,

a→2-a→1=-6i^+6j^+6k^-λi^+26j^-26k^=-6+λi^-6j^+36k^

b→1×b→2=i^j^k^345234

⇒b→1×b→2=i^-2j^+k^

⇒b→1×b→2=6

We know that the shortest distance between two skew lines is a→2-a→1·b→1×b→2b→1×b→2=6

⇒-6-λi^-6j^+36k^·i^-2j^+k^6=6

⇒46-λ6=6

⇒λ=106, -26

So, required sum is

=106-262=384

Now,

-2≤sinθ+cosθ≤2

But n∈Z, so n=-1, 0, 1

θ∈0,π2,3π4,7π4,3π2,π

So,

∑θ∈Ssin2θ+π4=12+12+0+0+12+12=2

since β→1 is parallel to α→, β1→=tα→

⇒β1→=t4i^+3j^+5k^=4ti^+3tj^+5tk^ .........2

β2→=i^+2j^-4k^-4ti^+3tj^+5tk^=1-4ti^+2-3tj^+-4-5tk^ ............3

⇒41-4t+32-3t+5-4-5t=0

⇒4-16t+6-9t-20-25t=0

⇒-50t=10⇒t=-15

From 2 and 3, we get  β1→=-154i^+3j^+5k^

β2→=95i^+135j^-3k^=159i^+13j^-15k^

So, the value of 5β→2·i^+j^+k^ will be,

=5×15(9i^+13j^-15k^)·i^+j^+k^=9+13-15=7

solution:

a→×c→=b→×c→

⇒a→×c→-b→×c→=0

⇒a→-b→×c→=0

So, a→-b→∥c→, hence

a→-b→=μc→

where, μ is any scalar.

⇒-2i^+7j^+2λk^=μc→   ....1

Taking dot product with a→, we get

⇒-2+14+2λ2=7μ

⇒7μ=2λ2+12  ...2

Taking dot product with b→ in 1, we get

-6-35-2λ2=-432μ

⇒82+4λ2=43μ   ....3

Solving 2 & 3, we get

λ2=1, μ=2

So,

a→·b→=3-10-λ2=-8

⇒a→·b→=8

solution:

The separation between the plate becomes twice and the space between them is filled with a medium of dielectric constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math>. Then the capacitance becomes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi>pF</mi></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is____.
solution:
Capacitance of the parallel plate capacitor when air is present between the plates, C=ε0Ad=15 pF

Capacitance of the parallel plate capacitor when dielectric is filled in between the plates,

 C'=kε0Ad'=3.5ε0A2d=3.52×15 pF=1054 pF

Therefore, x=105.

question:

solution:
AM broadcasting is radio broadcasting using amplitude modulation (AM) transmissions.

AM Broadcast range → 540–1600 kHz 

FM broadcasting is the method of radio broadcasting that uses frequency modulation (FM).

FM Broadcast range →88 – 108 MHz

question: A cell of emf <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>90</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math> is connected across series combination of two resistors each of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> resistance. A voltmeter of resistance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>400</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> is used to measure the potential difference across each resistor. The reading of the voltmeter will be:
solution:
As we know, voltmeter is connected in parallel to the element being measured.

The equivalent resistance of the above circuit is Req=400×100400+100+100=80+100=180 Ω

Current through the circuit is i=90180=12 A

Reading of the voltmeter is VR=i400×100400+100=12×80=40 V

solution:
Before heating, resistance of wire is R0=ρl0A0

After heating, resistance is R=ρlA

As volume = constant

Therefore,

A0l0=1.2l0A

⇒A=A01.2

So,

q:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I: Acceleration due to earth's gravity decreases as you go 'up' or 'down' from earth's surface.

Statement II: Acceleration due to earth's gravity is same at a height 'h' and depth 'd' from earth's surface, if h = d.

In the light of above statements, choose the most appropriate answer form the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I: Acceleration due to earth's gravity decreases as you go 'up' or 'down' from earth's surface.

Statement II: Acceleration due to earth's gravity is same at a height 'h' and depth 'd' from earth's surface, if h = d.

In the light of above statements, choose the most appropriate answer form the options given below
options: Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect
Both Statement I and II are correct
solution:
At any height, g'=g1-2hR[Given: h≪R]

and at any depth,

g'=g1-dR

Clearly, g' decreases in both cases. But if h=d, then value of g' will not be the same.

Therefore, statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.

q:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R

Assertion A: A pendulum clock when taken to Mount Everest becomes fast.

Reason R: The value of g (acceleration due to gravity) is less at Mount Everest than its value on the surface of earth.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R

Assertion A: A pendulum clock when taken to Mount Everest becomes fast.

Reason R: The value of g (acceleration due to gravity) is less at Mount Everest than its value on the surface of earth.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> are correct and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math>

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> are correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is not the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math>
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is not correct
solution:
Time period of a pendulum is given by T=2πLg, where L is the length of the pendulum and g is the acceleration due to gravity.

As, T∝1g, so the time period of vibration of the pendulum clock increases i.e. it starts taking more time to complete each vibration. Therefore, the pendulum clock becomes slow.

The acceleration due to gravity decreases with height h h≪R above the surface as:

g'=g1-hR 

Thus, value of g is less at Mount Everest than its value on the surface of earth.

Hence, assertion is wrong, but reason is correct.
